what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in the dragnet-development channel: a design feedback point from Tim about a vertical-drag level-selector interaction, followed by Tim's own reply agreeing that haptics and a preview indicator are important.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies it as a note authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel, commenting on board://item-89d62e5d
The body quotes a [point] arguing that vertical drag for scale-change conflicts with page scrolling and requires an unambiguous long-press latch, haptic confirmation, and a live outline preview
It mentions iOS PWA haptic support via the Vibration API and suggests borrowing Apple's long-press 'lift' pattern
Tim's reply says the selector was intended to activate only after a selection and to include a visual indicator, and he welcomes the haptics/preview refinements
standouts: The requirement is framed as non-negotiable: crisp latch + per-level haptic tick + live outline preview
It explicitly references Apple's lift pattern and iOS Vibration API for PWAs as implementation guidance
Tim's reply shifts the idea from a rough concept to a refined interaction with haptic and preview affordances
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a commented_on link to relate this note to its parent item
Markdown body separates a quoted external point from the author's reply
concerns: Tim's reply contains hedging language ('I don't know', 'or something') that leaves the exact activation and release behavior underspecified
No evidence that iOS Vibration API behavior has been verified on the target device
No code symbols or implementation details are present
notes: A design note insisting that a vertical-drag level selector must have a felt mode latch and live preview, with Tim agreeing haptics and preview are essential.
questions: What is the exact content of the parent item-89d62e5d?
What UI element or domain does the 'level-selector' control?
Has the iOS Vibration API haptic behavior been verified on the actual target devices?
What is the precise gesture for activation and decommit (lift, drag, release)?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a posted note/comment, which matches the actual content: a design point and reply.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to harden an informal vertical-drag idea into a reliable interaction by adding non-negotiable feedback mechanisms, while recording Tim's buy-in to those refinements.
novelty: It treats haptic ticks plus a live outline preview as mandatory rather than optional for a vertical drag scale selector, and explicitly ties this to Apple's lift pattern and iOS PWA Vibration API support.
contribution: Keeps the design constraint that a vertical-drag level selector must provide a crisp long-press latch, per-level haptic feedback, and a live outline preview; also keeps the pointer to iOS Vibration API support and the Apple lift-pattern precedent as implementation references.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on a vertical-drag level-selector design, arguing that without a crisp long-press latch, per-level haptic ticks, and a live outline preview the gesture will collide with scrolling and cause mis-commits, and acknowledging haptics and preview as key to quality.
intention_for_embedding: To record and reinforce non-negotiable interaction requirements for a vertical drag level-selector and capture Tim's agreement to refine the idea with haptics and preview.
